===''Pokémon the Series''=== [[File:Saturn anime.png|thumb|250px|Saturn in {{aniseries|JN}}]] ====''[[Pokémon the Series: Diamond and Pearl]]''==== Saturn debuted in a cameo at the end of ''[[DP036|A Secret Sphere of Influence!]]''. He, along with some {{tc|Team Galactic Grunt}}s, commented that [[Jessie]], [[James]], and {{MTR}} did a lousy job at stealing the [[Adamant Orb]] for them, and framing [[Nando]] for the crime. Saturn's first speaking appearance was in ''[[DP060|Journey to the Unown!]]'', along with his {{p|Bronzor}}. He managed to steal a cube that was under the guard of the {{p|Unown}} of the [[Solaceon Ruins]] and brought it with him back to the [[Team Galactic HQ]]. Later, in ''[[DP069|Enter Galactic!]]'', Saturn exposed the cube to the meteorites in [[Veilstone City]], transforming it into the [[Spear Key]]. He and a few Team Galactic Grunts went to go steal the meteorites, but ended up meeting {{Ash}} and {{ashfr}} for the first time. He also battled {{an|Brock}} and his {{TP|Brock|Croagunk}} with his own {{TP|Saturn|Toxicroak}}, which ended after Team Galactic made a tactical retreat. In ''[[DP096|Losing Its Lustrous]]'' and ''[[DP097|Double Team Turnover!]]'', Saturn stole the [[Adamant Orb]] while it was being transported to the [[Celestic Town Historical Research Center]] from Eterna City. He also aided in stealing the [[Lustrous Orb]] from the [[Celestic Town Historical Research Center]] but was admonished by Cyrus after the completion of the mission for displaying ineptitude. In ''[[DP111|Saving the World From Ruins!]]'', following the failure of [[Mars]]'s [[Iron Island]] mission, [[Cyrus]] placed Saturn in charge of the [[Spear Pillar]]'s excavation from [[Mount Coronet]]. He worked with [[Charon]] to locate ruins on Mount Coronet in ''[[DP136|Gateway to Ruin!]]''. Team Galactic located some ruins, though not the ones they wanted. Saturn and the others then fled following the arrival of [[Looker]] and Ash's group. Later, Saturn returned to Mount Coronet and discovered the Spear Pillar ruins in ''[[DP150|Unlocking the Red Chain of Events!]]'', allowing him to fast-track Team Galactic's scheme. In ''[[DP151|The Needs of the Three!]]'', Saturn kept {{Gary}} at bay while [[J|Pokémon Hunter J]] successfully captured the three {{an|lake guardians}} at [[Lake Valor]]. He later returned to [[Team Galactic HQ]]. In ''[[DP152|The Battle Finale of Legend!]]'', Saturn's Toxicroak was defeated by Brock's Croagunk. After Cyrus passed through the [[dimension]]al portal created by {{p|Dialga}} and {{p|Palkia}} he realized that Cyrus had just been using him and the other {{tc|Commander}}s to reach his own goals and cared little for those who served him. When Mars hysterically attempted to follow their Leader, Saturn grabbed her arm to block her from doing so, shaking his head solemnly at his fellow Commander. Afterwards he was arrested along with Mars and [[Jupiter]] by [[Looker]] and stated that Team Galactic was finished. He appeared briefly in ''[[DP191|Memories are Made of Bliss!]]'' in {{TRT}}'s fantasy with all the other members of Team Galactic. ====''[[Pokémon Journeys: The Series]]''==== Saturn reappeared in ''[[JNS01|The Arceus Chronicles (Part 1)]]'' through ''[[JNS04|The Arceus Chronicles (Part 4)]]'', leading the resurrected Team Galactic in an attempt to bring back Cyrus. As the first step of the operation, he successfully {{pkmn2|caught}} a {{p|Heatran}} in order to power a device meant to open a gate to the world that Cyrus had disappeared into. The operation, however, experienced a setback when the energy cannon exploded and Heatran went on a rampage. Saturn and the other Commanders later faced Ash and his friends in order to stop them from interfering with Team Galactic's plan, but the Commanders were ultimately defeated and captured by {{an|Cynthia}} and placed back under arrest by [[Officer Jenny]], ending their plan in failure. ====Character==== Saturn is determined to successfully complete all his missions and would order a tactical retreat to avoid further complications. As a result of this perfectionist quality of his personality, Saturn tended to call out his {{TP|Saturn|Toxicroak}} to handle interruptions like {{Ash}} and {{ashfr}}. ====Pokémon==== This listing is of Saturn's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}: =====On hand===== {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Saturn |pkmn=Bronzor |type1=Steel |type2=Psychic |img=Saturn Bronzor.png |epnum=DP060 |epname=Journey to the Unown! |vajp=Kiyotaka Furushima |vaen=Tom Wayland |desc={{p|Bronzor}} first appeared in ''[[DP060|Journey to the Unown!]]'', where it guided Saturn and his squad through the [[Solaceon Ruins]]. It was also responsible for confusing the {{p|Unown}} living in the ruins which resulted in them all running wild. In ''[[DP096|Losing Its Lustrous!]]'', Bronzor proved instrumental in Team Galactic's plan to steal the [[Lustrous Orb]] by putting the security teams guarding it to sleep. It was then defeated in battle by {{an|Cynthia}}'s {{TP|Cynthia|Garchomp}}. Bronzor's known moves are {{m|Flash}}, {{m|Confuse Ray}}, and {{m|Hypnosis}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Saturn |pkmn=Toxicroak |main=yes |type1=Poison |type2=Fighting |img=Saturn Toxicroak.png |epnum=DP069 |epname=Enter Galactic! |vajp=Shin-ichiro Miki |vaen=Bill Rogers |desc={{p|Toxicroak}} first appeared in ''[[DP069|Enter Galactic!]]'', where it helped to steal the [[Veilstone City]] meteorites. During the battle, it developed a fierce rivalry with {{an|Brock}}'s {{TP|Brock|Croagunk}}. It has since become Saturn's main Pokémon.}} =====Escaped===== {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Saturn |pkmn=Heatran |type1=Fire |type2=Steel |img=Saturn Heatran.png |epnum=JNS01 |epname=The Arceus Chronicles (Part 1) |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen=Kenta Miyake |desc={{p|Heatran}} first appeared in ''[[JNS01|The Arceus Chronicles (Part 1)]]'', where Saturn caught it with a [[Dusk Ball]]. He brought it to the [[Team Galactic HQ]], where used it, along with a [[Flame Plate]], to power a device that Team Galactic was using in an effort to bring back [[Cyrus]]. However, in ''[[JNS02|The Arceus Chronicles (Part 2)]]'', the machine overloaded, and Heatran was struck by a burst of flames from it. This triggered its Flash Fire Ability and sent it on a rampage, melting its own Dusk Ball and swallowing the Flame Plate. Heatran's known moves are {{m|Stone Edge}} and {{m|Magma Storm}}, and its Ability is {{a|Flash Fire}}.}} ====Voice actors==== {{vatable|color=7394D5|bordercolor=8bb2ff |ja=岡村明美 ''[[Akemi Okamura]]'' |en=[[Marc Thompson]] |nl=Florus van Rooijen |ar=عماد فغالي ''Imad Feghaly'' ([[JNS01]]-[[JNS04]]) |cs=Jan Maxián ([[DP060]]-[[DP111]])
